content_markdown: The molar mass of water is $18\text{ g}$. The approximate number of water molecules in a glass of water is
vibe_explanation: To estimate this, consider a standard glass of water holds about $250\text{ ml}$, which is approximately $250\text{ g}$ of water. Since the molar mass is $18\text{ g/mol}$, the number of moles is roughly $250 / 18 \approx 14\text{ moles}$. Multiplying by Avogadro's constant ($6 \times 10^{23}\text{ molecules/mol}$) gives $14 \times 6 \times 10^{23} \approx 8.4 \times 10^{24}$, which is closest to the order of magnitude $10^{25}$.
